items.previewTitle:
Trailer drop!! 🎉

Please enjoy the trailer for Take The Fall. We’re so happy to finally share this with you all, a lot of hard work and passion has been put into it. 

We’d also like to once again thank everyone who was involved with or supported the making of this project along the way. We could not have made the film without you, and it would not be as spectacular without the numerous hours and immense energy you put into your time on or behind the screen 

Make sure to be following us to stay up to date with our posts… exciting news coming soon… 🌟🌟

‘Jason is on the run after going against the rules of the special forces, Sam, his prodigy, tracks him down to confront him about what he has done...only to discover there is more at play than the special forces HQ let on.’

🎬💥

sharedPost.text:
---

🌀🎬🎥🌚 Exclusive Acting Workshop – Final Version .version A.

VERY LOW COST: One-on-One Online via Skype

"This course integrates the poetic realism of Tarkovsky, the practical wisdom of Michael Caine, and the transformative techniques of Stanislavski and Method Acting. Designed to deepen your craft, it focuses on authentic emotional connection and powerful storytelling."

🎬 Created by a British Cinematographer/Director with 31 Years of Experience and 32 Features to His Name

🌐 International Experience: Having worked on films worldwide, collaborating with actors and directors, I bring real-world insights from both sides of the camera. This course is designed to give actors a deep understanding of their craft, emphasizing emotional depth and character development.

🌐 IMDb: www.imdb.com/name/nm1806508

🌐 Showreel: https://enkiproductions1.8b.io

---

🤔 What You Will Learn:

🌀 Emotional Range: Master the ability to express a full spectrum of emotions, making your performances more believable and compelling.

🌀 Character Development: Create rich, multidimensional characters by exploring their internal world, external actions, and backstory.

🌀 Method Acting: Use personal experiences and emotions to deliver raw, authentic performances.

🌀 Working with Directors and Crew: Learn how to hit marks, understand shooting ratios, and collaborate with crew members to enhance your performance.

🌀 Scene Preparation: Techniques for script interpretation, emotional connection, and performing under pressure.

🌀 Industry Standards: How to create a professional showreel and work effectively with casting directors and filmmakers.

---

🎬 Day 1: Moment-to-Moment Acting (Stanislavski’s Approach)

🔹 Breaking a Scene Down: The use of internal visualization and meditation to "become" the character.

Character Exercise: The Hitman’s First Kill

📍 Scene: A low-rent hotel room in Manhattan, lit by a dull winter’s midday light. The sad hum of a ceiling fan, distant sirens, muted traffic below. On the bed—a brand-new sniper rifle.

🔹 Exercise 1 (Singular Emotion): Enter the room, pick up the gun, examine it, and prepare for the target. Remain fully in character.

🔹 Exercise 2 (Multiple Emotional Shifts):

1. Enter slightly out of breath, feeling relief—"I made it here. No one saw me."

2. The weight of reality sets in—"Am I really about to kill someone?"

3. Mesmerized by the artistry of the gun—draw on memories of seeing/touching weapons.

4. Snapped back to reality by a siren—trace the feeling of waking from a dream.

5. Decision made—move to the window, set up the gun, and wait.

🔹 Discussion: How do actors like Heath Ledger, Daniel Day-Lewis, and Dustin Hoffman make their characters so real? Insights from my own experiences on set with actors who never broke character—even during dinner.

Exercise 3 (If Time Allows): The Phone Call

📍 Scene: You’ve done something terrible—enough to make the person you love most leave forever. You must convince them to stay.

🎭 Goal: Improvise, using pure emotional truth, until the performance is undeniably real.

---

🎬 Day 2: Making Your Character Believable

🔹 Understanding the Psyche: Whether playing a killer or an ordinary person, you must know your character’s mind.

Character Exercise: The Breaking Point

📍 Scene: A crowded supermarket. A tired voice announces deals over the PA. You feel unseen, unheard, frustrated—until suddenly, you’re holding an AK-47. (Note: These scenes are purely acting exercises. They can be altered if a student is uncomfortable.)

🔹 Alternative Scene: A character about to detonate a bomb, written in the style of Heath Ledger’s Joker.

🔹 Alternative Scene: A video call to the person you love most—telling them why. (Scripted or improv.)

📍 Final Monologue Exercise: "Hi everyone… Tomorrow’s going to be different." The last words before an irreversible decision.

🔹 Accents: Versatility in performance—working with British, American (Deep South), and Northern Irish accents.

🔹 Final Exercise: The Power of Stillness—sit before the camera, smiling, at total peace. Convey pure serenity.

---

🎬 Day 3: Working with a Director and Crew

🔹 Understanding Your Role: How an actor fits into the filmmaking process.

🔹 Hitting Marks & Lighting: The technical side of making performances shine.

🔹 Shooting Ratios: How they affect your performance.

🔹 Crew Collaboration: Understanding continuity, sound, makeup, cinematography, and direction.

Final Showreel Scenes (Day 3):

🎭 Sinister: "So you really think you got away with it? You really think that?" (Improv)

🎭 Heartbroken: "But I didn’t mean it... please, please, don’t leave me…" (Improv)

🎭 Insane (Joker-like): "Mmm... if E = mc², then time versus chaos... who cares?" (Improv)

🎭 Flat & Emotionless: "We’re born, we live, we die. That’s it." (Improv)

🔹 Goal: Draw from personal experiences and trace memories to make the performances deeply real.

---

🎬 Day 4: Fear and Survival – Personal Experience & Character Creation

🔹 Deep Dive: Exploring fear, survival, and desperation in character work.

Exercise 1: The Jungle Escape

📍 Scene: You’re lost in the jungle at night, exhausted, your breath the only sound. Every step is agony, but stopping means death. You call out—pleading for help. No answer.

🎭 Emotions to Explore:

1. Fear of death – the realization that no one is coming.

2. Desperation – the raw instinct to survive.

3. Physical exhaustion – feeling your body give in.

🎭 Method Process:

1. Internal Visualization – Close your eyes, hear the insects, the rustling leaves, your pounding heartbeat.

2. Personal Connection – Recall a moment when you felt completely lost or afraid.

3. Scene Execution – Let the emotions guide your body and voice.

Exercise 2: The Phone Call Redux

📍 Scene: You’re on the phone with a loved one. You need their help. If they refuse, it’s over. What do you say? What do you leave out?

🎭 Objective: Make the desperation feel real. This is life or death.

---

Final Performance (Day 4):

Students will perform a final emotional scene, bringing together everything they’ve learned. These performances will be recorded as raw material for their showreel.

---

🎬🎥 Ready to Elevate Your Acting?

This isn’t just a class—it’s an intense, immersive experience that will change the way you act forever.

🌐 To Enroll: p.m.me or e.mail:

mikeenkiproductions1@gmail.com

Let’s create something unforgettable.

items.text:
Hi there! Just wanted some advice on this, I’m a drama graduate and I really want to get into the casting and talent management side of the industry. The dream is to become a casting assistant/PA and work my way up to be a casting director one day! I’m based in the North West but all the vacancies I’ve been seeing on CGD and PMA have all been London based. 
Please let me know if any tips or anyone I can get into contact with regarding this. 
Thank you in advance!

topComments.text: Speak to LA productions and other independent companies. They might have some work experience vacancies.

# **CASTING CALL: SEASALT - IPF/Bell Series**
* Shooting in PEI & Cape Breton.
* Paid plus travel
* Casting for both Union & Non-Union.
* May-June 2026

Kraków, szukam ludzi do spektaklu

Poszukuję aktorek i aktorów (niekoniecznie profesjonalnych) do autorskiego spektaklu opartego na zmyślonych życiorysach trzech sióstr. Szukam dziewczyn do ról: Siostry - śpiewaczki, Siostry - malarski i Siostry - pielęgniarki, a także dwóch-trzech facetów do ról epizodycznych. Premiera spektaklu: początek maja. Projekt z założenia niekomercyjny, chociaż nie wiadomo, gdzie nas może poprowadzić. Proszę o kontakt na priv. Pozdrawiam.

items.text:
Narodowy Teatr Edukacji im. Adama Mickiewicza we Wrocławiu zaprasza na rozmowy sezonowe chętnych do udziału w spektaklach naszego Teatru, realizowanych w sezonie 2026/27. 

Poszukujemy zarówno uzdolnionych amatorów, jak i aktorów z wykształceniem kierunkowym (AST, Studium Teatralne, Szkoły Artystyczne ROE, Szkoła Aktorska LART itp.)

Przedstawienia będą grane na terenie całego kraju - od września bieżącego roku, do czerwca 2027.

WYMAGANIA:

- wiek:  18 – 40 lat

- dyspozycyjność

- dobry stan zdrowia

- mile widziane dodatkowe umiejętności - wokalne i taneczne

OFERUJEMY:

- zatrudnienie na sezon w formie umowy-zlecenia/umowy o dzieło w ustalonym wymiarze (2,3 lub 4 tygodnie w miesiącu)

- gwarancję pracy (działamy nieprzerwanie od ponad 25 lat)

- elastyczny czas pracy

- atrakcyjne zarobki

- opcję ubezpieczenia zdrowotnego

- możliwość osobistego rozwoju artystycznego w profesjonalnym zespole teatralnym

- ambitny repertuar teatralny (głównie klasyka polska i europejska)

- okres prób do spektakli (sierpień-połowa września, WROCŁAW) dodatkowo gratyfikowany

Powyższa oferta dotyczy oddziału siedziby głównej Teatru we Wrocławiu.

Zgłoszenia i CV prosimy wysyłać do Dyrektora Artystycznego Marzenny Kucmer-Kołeńskiej na adres:

 teatr.zgloszenia@gmail.com

items.text:
!!CASTING CALL!!

Male – Black (18-22)

Female – Black (22-30)

Characters

Nate Fincher: Still dealing with the death of his mother and the guilt that comes along with grief. He wishes to go back and change the outcome, even though he knows he can’t.

Teri Fincher: Nate’s older sister, she’s a hospital worker who’s using work as a way to keep herself busy.

This film will be shooting in the Metro-Detroit area March 6th-8th later in the day.

Dreamology: Nate sits in front of the TV alone, again. At first, it seems like another stale night. Enter, Dream·ol·o·gy, a late night show hosted by the indoctrinated Jeremy Pickett. During the show Nate learns of an ethereal concept known as dream-phazing, which allows you to enter an altered state of dreaming. Why? To revisit past memories in hopes of experiencing new outcomes. What Nate soon learns however, is that the line between dreams and reality can be easily blurred, especially when paired with unresolved grief. Will he wake up?

items.text:
For LA Actresses & Creatives

Preparing for castings, self-tapes, or on-set work?

If your wardrobe doesn’t reflect the roles you’re ready to book, I offer structured style alignment sessions focused on:

• Defining your casting range visually
• Silhouette and proportion analysis
• Character-aligned outfit formulas
• A focused shopping direction
• A cohesive off-screen image

This is a strategy-based session designed to help you show up with clarity, intention, and confidence.

Shopping and sourcing are available separately.

DM me if you’d like more details.
